campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review the city's finances for July 2025, including how the city's invested money performed and the overall budget status. They also discussed plans to improve transportation options around Campbell, including ways to make it easier for people to get around using different methods like walking, biking, and public transit.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment performance and financial transactions
Monthly budget and treasurer's financial report
Campbell Multimodal Transportation Plan to improve walking, biking, and transit options

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council held a public hearing to get community feedback on a proposed new rule or law. After listening to residents, the council decided to move forward with the proposal and skip the usual first formal reading, planning to vote on it at their next meeting instead.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident input
New ordinance being fast-tracked for adoption
First reading waived to speed up the process
Vote scheduled for next regular council meeting

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — March 17, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met to approve routine administrative items and review important city plans. The council approved financial reports, housing and development plans, and community health services for the year ahead, including approval of a new residential development project called Solana.
keyTopics: Approved the Solana Project residential development (Tract 10657)
Reviewed January 2026 city finances and investments
Accepted annual progress report on housing and general city planning
Approved 2026 work plan for community health and senior services
Updated council meeting minute-taking procedures

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 1, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to organize upcoming council sessions scheduled for April 7 and April 14, 2026. They reviewed what items should be added to the agendas, determined which topics needed specific time slots, and checked that all required public notices were properly filed.
keyTopics: Finalized the April 7, 2026 council meeting agenda
Planned the April 14, 2026 draft agenda
Scheduled specific time slots for agenda items
Verified public notice requirements were met
Reviewed public records from March 19-26, 2026

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 2, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council's Human Relations Committee meeting scheduled for April 2, 2026 has been canceled. Residents should mark their calendars for the next meeting, which will be held on May 7, 2026.
keyTopics: Human Relations Committee meeting canceled
Next meeting scheduled for May 7, 2026
No specific agenda items discussed at this session

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to review funding applications for programs that help with housing and community development in Sunnyvale. The group approved minutes from their previous meeting and heard presentations from organizations seeking federal grant money to support local housing and human services projects.
keyTopics: Federal grant funding opportunities for housing and community programs
Review of applications from organizations seeking CDBG funds
Approval of February meeting minutes
Community development and human services initiatives

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 6, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a meeting on April 6, 2026 where residents were invited to speak about any city matters for up to 3 minutes each. The main business included electing new leaders for the Legislative Review Committee for 2026.
keyTopics: Public comment period for residents on city matters
Election of Legislative Review Committee Chair and Vice Chair
2026 legislative calendar planning
State law limits on decisions made about non-agenda items

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 1, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara's Audit Committee held a meeting to review a report about how Silicon Valley Power handled its power purchase agreements. The city is making these audit meetings available both in person and online so residents can participate however works best for them.
keyTopics: Silicon Valley Power's power purchase agreement process under review
Audit Committee meetings now offered as hybrid (in-person and remote)
City Auditor's Office report on Power Purchase Agreement
Public participation options via Zoom or phone conference

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — March 17, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met on March 17, 2026, to discuss a new program that would help residents get security cameras for their homes through a grant initiative. The council also reviewed the city's spending plans for the next five years (2027-2031), including what infrastructure projects and improvements are planned.
keyTopics: Home security camera grant program for residents
Five-year capital improvement projects (2027-2031)
City budget and financial planning for next five years
Approval of previous meeting minutes and upcoming meeting schedule

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 31, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to approve the city's gas utility budget and rates for the next fiscal year. The main decision involved updating how much residents and businesses pay for natural gas service, with different rate changes for residential customers, commercial buildings, and large industrial users.
keyTopics: Gas utility rates increasing for FY 2027
Separate rate changes for residential, commercial, and large business customers
Transfer of reserves and general fund money to cover utility operations
One hour of discussion and voting on the proposal (6:35 PM - 7:35 PM)
